mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.24% as of 2021-06-15 indicates ultra-loose monetary stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month employment growth measured by PAYEMS exceeding 200,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 143,242 thousand as of 2021-06-15 reflects post-pandemic recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of instances where PAYEMS monthly change exceeds 200,000, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales measured by RSAFS accelerating above 0.5% month-over-month signals strengthening consumer demand and forward earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 448,086 million as of 2021-06-15 reflects elevated consumer spending
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production measured by INDPRO declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness or demand destruction that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.02 as of 2021-06-15 reflects post-pandemic production recovery
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in less than 48% of triggered instances, or the mechanism shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate measured by UNRATE combined with declining payroll employment growth signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 1 month AND PAYEMS monthly change < 100,000
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% and PAYEMS at 143,242 thousand as of 2021-06-15
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of jointly triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST declining below 1,100 thousand units combined with rising mortgage rates signals weakening housing demand and reduced forward economic growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1,100 thousand AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5pp in 3 months
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing demand weakness
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in less than 48% of jointly triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerating above 0.5% month-over-month signals rising price pressures that force the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 238.111 as of 2021-05-15 reflects post-pandemic inflation pressures
regime_hint: inflationary environment with monetary policy tightening risk
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in less than 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP measured by GDPC1 growing above 2% annualized combined with accelerating retail sales signals robust economic expansion that supports equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2% AND RSAFS quarterly growth > 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,414 billion as of 2021-01-15 reflects post-pandemic recovery
regime_hint: strong economic expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of jointly triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
